architecture.tool_evidence: FRAMEWORK-OWNED. Harness CRG re-ran via crg_independent.py at finalize_gate → community_cohesion.score=64.7 (architecture_score=64.7). Healthy=11, Unhealthy=6, Total=17, Excluded=8 test communities. Unhealthy: workflows-aprompt(0.196/23), taskq-cmd(0.212/14), workflows-json(0.155/13), taskq-tasks(0.260/9), integration-test-verify(0.206/6), taskq-breaker(0.239/6). Root cause: src/taskq/ should be ONE production community per SAD.md §2.1 Principle 1, but Leiden splits it into 6 taskq-* sub-communities (cli.py command handlers, store.py, breaker.py, cache.py, executor.py, parser) with low intra-community edge density. Workflow files (.claude/workflows/*.js) and integration-test-verify (root-level *.mjs) are not auto-excluded by path-based detection (their files are not under /tests/ or /.methodology/). NOT the orchestrator false-positive (which yields score=0); this is genuine low-cohesion from sparse intra-module call graphs in a small (7 file) Python package. Threshold=80; BLOCKED.

findings.message: taskq-cmd community cohesion 0.212 < 0.3 (14 nodes, undirected internal-edge density too low for src/taskq/ to remain a single Leiden community).
workflows-aprompt (cohesion 0.196) and workflows-json (cohesion 0.155) communities not auto-excluded (path-based detection only excludes /tests/ and /.methodology/).
integration-test-verify (cohesion 0.206) not auto-excluded by path-based detection (file at project root, not under /tests/ or /.methodology/).

test_assertion_quality.tool_evidence: ast-assertions scanner over 03-development/tests/*.py -> 63 test funcs, 60 asserted, 3 zero-assert. Round-3 fix: test_nfr03_atomic_write_kill9_recovery was previously zero-assert (used pytest.fail inside except); converted to explicit assert (validated count + non-falsy decode) - now asserted. Remaining 3 zero-assert are pytest-benchmark fixtures in test_perf_nfr01.py - by-design: pytest-benchmark measures via the `benchmark` fixture, no assert needed. Framework score formula: 100 x asserted/total = 100 x 60/63 = 95.24.
